t:
Room306 . phoneb4g-546b Stt
Office of
Deferred Compensation
and
Defined Contribution
Plan & Trust
August 30, L990
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95S04-08O7
ATTN: Legal Department
RE: Filing Reguirements
Form 721-
Greetings:
After a telephone conversation with Ms. Peggy
Bernardi of your office on August 29, 1990, I arn writing
this letter for a formal opinion on the reguirement or
obligation to file Form 721, Statement of Economic
Interests.
My situation is as follows: I am staffing a
temporary deferred compensation advisory committee. This
committee will be made up of five to nine county employees
whose knowledge, background and interest are relevant to
lending sound analysis and advice to the deferred
compensation plan administrator. These individuals are
invited by the PIan Administrator to serve as a committee
member. The sole objective of this committee is to solicit
Request for Proposals to prospective investment providers
for -.he Couaty t s deferred conpensatj-on assets. The1, r^ri Il-
then review a1I submitted proposals and make a
recommendation to the plan administrator on a selection of
one or more investment providers for deferred compensation
assets. The Plan Administrator will take their
reconmendation into consideration, however he witl take his
own recommendation (which will probably be the
reconmendation subrnitted by the committee) to the Board of
Supervisors for adoption. Once a recommendation has been
made to the Plan Administrator, the duties of the committee
have been concluded and the committee will be disbanded.


Page 2
FPPC
As I am sure you are aware, Internal Revenue Code 457
deferred compensation assets are considered assets of the
enpLoyer (the county) until they are distributed to the
employee. The PLan Administrator of the deferred
cornpensation program is also the county Treasurer - who is
required to file a FORM 721. The question Lies in whether
the committee is also required or obligated to file a FoRM
721- because they are rendering advice to the PIan
Administrator.
The language in the preface to the instruction rnanual
for FORM 721 says that:
'r...A1I other public officials 
and employees who
manage public investnents including but not lirnited
to:
Employees anC ccnsultants vhc have decision
rnakinq authoritv with reqard to investnent of
city, countv or state funds. This includes,
but is not limited to, pension fund managers
for public agencies, as well as anv person to
whom a city or countv treasurer has deleqated
such authoritv... rt (emphasis added)
I directed this question to our county counsef and
the response was that these committee members wouLd
t'probably be required" to file a FoRM 721,. This answer i.s
based on a very broad interpretation of the present
information available as stated above. counsel opines that
since the cornmittee is rnaking a recommendation regarding an
investrnent provider, they indirectly have decision making
authority by virtue of the delegation to review and make a
reconmendation on $rho $rould handle the investrnent of county
funds .
This is the second appointnent of a deferred
compensation advisory cornmittee. The first conmittee
appointment in the fa11 of 1987. The recommendations frorn
the first 
committee were accepted by the Plan
Adrninistrator.
Specifical,Iy, my questions are:
l-. 
Are the temporary deferred compensation advisory
cornmj-ttee members required to file a Form 72L?
2. 
If yes, are they reguired to file both 'rentering
officetr and a ttleaving office" forms at the beginning and
concl-usion of their committee duties (which could last for
up to 6 months at the most).


Page 3
FPPC
3. 
If no, are they required to file another conflict of
interest or statement of economi-c interest form nohr?
4. 
If no, will future committee menbers be required to
file a Form 721 or other form j,n tfre future (taking into
consideration that every three to five years a committee
rr/iII probably be appointed to evaluate potential investment
providers for deferred compensation assets.
During our phone conversation, Ils. Bernardi
seemed to think that because this committee has only
perforrned on one other occasion, we have not yet set a
precedent as to being required to file a Forn 721.
Further, because the connittee only provides a
recommendation to the PIan Adninistrator, and it is not
binding, that the comrnittee members have not been afforded
the authority to nake decisj,ons regarding the investment of
public funds.
I look forward to your pronpt response to the above
questions. If you have any guestions, you may reach me at
805-549-5465, in the office of Retirement Plans.
Very truly yours,
,) 
', ',-'. /''
Andrea Paley
Pension Analyst
Staff to Deferred
compensation committee
cc: Frank L. Freitas
Plan Administrator
